channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Western Cape High Court rules Cape Town’s value-linked service charges unlawful
item.description: On 30 April 2026, the Western Cape High Court ruled that Cape Town's fixed charges for citywide cleaning, water, and sanitation, which were based on property value, were unlawful and unconstitutional....

item.title: Leila de Lima proposes graduated wealth tax on billionaires in the Philippines
item.description: ML Party-list Rep. Leila de Lima has filed a bill in the House of Representatives seeking to impose an annual “ultra-wealth tax” on billionaires in the Philippines. The proposed measure, House Bill No...

item.title: Trump Seeks Control Over Presidential Records to Block Future Investigations
item.description: Former and current U.S. President Donald Trump is actively working to claim personal ownership over his presidential records, a move legal experts warn could create a lasting legal barrier against inv...

item.title: Ukraine denies attacking Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant amid continued strikes on Russian energy infrastructure
item.description: Ukraine has denied Russian accusations that it struck the Russian-controlled Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant, even as it continues to intensify drone attacks on energy infrastructure inside Russia. A...

item.title: TANAPA introduces redesigned entry permits to improve park management and tourism services
item.description: Tanzania National Parks Authority (TANAPA) has officially launched redesigned entry permits aimed at improving tourism services and strengthening management across the country’s protected areas. The n...

item.title: Karachi introduces e-challan system for lane discipline enforcement on Sharea Faisal starting June 1
item.description: The Karachi Traffic Police have announced the launch of an electronic challan (e-challan) system targeting lane violations on Sharea Faisal, one of the city’s busiest arteries, effective from June 1. ...

item.title: Critical reflection on Tinubu’s three years in power and Nigeria’s political culture
item.description: This opinion piece by Festus Adedayo take serious look at President Bola Tinubu’s third year in office, using Yoruba proverbs, cultural symbols, and strong political criticism to explain how power in ...

item.title: RM reflects on BTS members’ emotional strain during military service hiatus ahead of reunion
item.description: RM, leader of BTS, has spoken candidly about the emotional difficulties experienced by the group during their hiatus while completing mandatory military service. The band paused activities in 2022 as ...

item.title: Ralf Schumacher marries Etienne Bousquet-Cassagne in St Tropez ceremony
item.description: Former Formula 1 driver Ralf Schumacher has married his partner Etienne Bousquet-Cassagne in a lavish ceremony held in St Tropez, France. The wedding took place as part of a three-day celebration atte...

item.title: DSS Operative Killed, Two Others Injured During Oil Bunkering Gang Attack in Imo Community
item.description: For Imo State, Abacheke community don witness serious wahala as suspected oil bunkering gang attack DSS operational base. The attack happen between late Friday night and early Saturday morning. Accord...

item.title: Critics challenge Trump’s interpretation of cognitive screening test results
item.description: The article reports on a wave of criticism following a late-night social media post by Donald Trump in which he claimed to have achieved a perfect score on a cognitive screening test and presented it ...

item.title: Nakuru court awards employee KSh 1.6 million after ruling her dismissal was procedurally unfair
item.description: A Nakuru court has awarded a senior accounts clerk, Margaret Karanja, over KSh 1.6 million after finding that her dismissal from Kentalya PLC was unlawful and procedurally unfair. Karanja, who earned ...

item.title: Ethiopia Advances Local Drug Manufacturing Through Stronger Regulations
item.description: Ethiopia is making big moves to improve its pharmaceutical sector by tightening regulations and boosting local drug production. The government wants to reduce dependence on imported medicines and make...

item.title: Rave reacts positively to Negi’s comment on Kapamilya Deal or No Deal
item.description: The entertainment segment from Kapamilya Deal or No Deal featured a light and engaging interaction between personalities Rave and Negi, which drew attention from viewers due to its humorous and lively...

item.title: Exploring Adolescence and Artistic Sensibilities in Denton Welch’s ‘In Youth is Pleasure’
item.description: Denton Welch’s novel, 'In Youth is Pleasure', first published in 1945 and republished by Penguin Classics in 2021, is a delicate and whimsical exploration of adolescent curiosity. The story revolves a...

item.title: Federal delegation visits Oyo following abduction of school pupils and teachers in Oriire LGA
item.description: A high-level Federal Government delegation led by the National Security Adviser, Nuhu Ribadu, alongside the Inspector-General of Police, Olatunji Disu, the Minister of Defence, Christopher Musa, and t...

item.title: Debate Emerges Over Inflation Metrics as Trump Fed Nominee Kevin Warsh Faces Criticism
item.description: A former insider has accused Donald Trump’s Federal Reserve chair nominee, Kevin Warsh, of using misleading methods to present inflation data in a more favorable light. The controversy stems from Wars...

item.title: Tanzania Smallholder Dairy Farmers Access Better Markets and Support Over a Million Families
item.description: Tanzania’s small-scale dairy farmers are now seeing better access to formal markets and modern facilities thanks to big improvements in the sector. For many years, farmers struggled with unreliable bu...

item.title: Angeles City hotel collapse death toll rises to 11 as retrieval operations continue
item.description: The death toll in the hotel collapse incident in Angeles City, Pampanga has risen to 11, according to the latest report. The tragic incident has prompted ongoing retrieval and recovery operations as a...

item.title: How football tracksuits help migrants preserve identity and community
item.description: Neal Corcoran, who migrated from Ireland to Australia in 1991, has built a unique personal and community story around football tracksuits, which he sees as far more than sportswear. Having grown up in...
